findings.summary: Decoupling observation incorrectly reports +9V has no bypass caps
findings.details: The design_observations entry for U1 (LM386) lists rails_without_caps=['+9V'] and rails_with_caps=[]. However, the schematic nets clearly show C3 (0.1µF disc cap) and C4 (100µF electrolytic) both connected to the +9V rail along with U1 pin 6 (V+). The decoupling_analysis list is also empty. The analyzer is failing to associate C3 and C4 with the +9V power rail when evaluating the LM386 decoupling coverage.

findings.summary: LM386 audio amplifier circuit not detected as amplifier or speaker-driver
findings.details: The LM386 is from the Amplifier_Audio KiCad library and is wired as a classic audio power amplifier: input at pin 3 (+), gain-setting cap C1 (10µF) across pins 1-8, bypass cap C2 on pin 7, Zobel network (R1+C5), output coupling cap C7 (470µF) to speaker connector J3. Despite the subcircuit being identified around U1, neither opamp_circuits nor buzzer_speaker_circuits contain any entries. The audio amplifier topology is a missed detection.

findings.summary: Courtyard overlap reported for U1/C1 with 0.0mm² overlap is a zero-area false positive
findings.details: The placement_analysis lists a courtyard overlap between U1 and C1 with overlap_mm2=0.0. A zero-area overlap is a boundary-touching condition, not an actual overlap, and should not be flagged as a courtyard violation. The three real overlaps (U1/R2 at 26.16mm², C5/R1 at 7.41mm², J3/U1 at 3.895mm²) are plausible given the compact 45.72×33.02mm board, but the 0.0mm² entry is a false positive.
